name: Baked Orzo with Mozzarella and Oregano

ingredients.item: large eggplant, cut into ¾-inch/2-cm dice (3⅔ cups/300 g)
medium carrots, peeled and cut into ⅔-inch/1.5-cm dice (2⅓ cups/300 g)
celery stalks, cut into ⅔-inch/1.5-cm dice (2 cups/200 g)
large onion, finely diced (scant 1¼ cups/170 g)
fresh oregano, chopped, or 1½ tbsp thyme leaves grated zest of 1 lemon
oz/120 g firm mozzarella, cut into ⅜-inch/1-cm dice
medium tomatoes, cut into slices ⅜-inch/1-cm thick (2¼ cups/400 g)
dried oregano salt and black pepper

method: Heat the olive oil in a large sauté pan over medium-high heat. Add the eggplant and cook for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden brown.

Remove with a slotted spoon to paper towels and set aside. Add the carrots and celery to the pan and fry for 8 minutes. Transfer to paper towels. Turn down the heat to medium and add the onion and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring often. Add the orzo and tomato paste and cook for a further 2 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat and add the stock, fresh oregano, and lemon zest. Add the cooked vegetables, mozzarella, Parmesan, 1 teaspoon salt, and
½ teaspoon pepper. Mix well and transfer to a rectangular baking dish, 8½ by
10½-inches (21 by 27-cm) or a round one, 10.5 inches (27 cm) in diameter.
Arrange the tomatoes on top and sprinkle with the dried oregano, ¼ teaspoon salt, and a grind of black pepper.
Bake for 40 minutes, until all the liquid has been absorbed and the pasta is cooked through. Remove, leave to settle for 5 minutes, and serve.
